medicinalProperties: 🌿 Prostate health - Used for benign prostatic hyperplasia treatment
🌿 Malaria treatment - Bark extracts used in traditional medicine
preparation: Ground into powder for tea or capsules
Decoction prepared by boiling bark in water

culturalContext.significance: Sacred species in Bakiga culture, used for over 200 years
culturalContext.traditionalKnowledge: Harvesting rituals performed by community elders during full moon

prunus_africana.conservation: Vulnerable - Protected under CITES Appendix II

medicinalProperties: 🌿 Nutritional supplement - Rich in vitamins A, C, E, and minerals
🌿 Anti-inflammatory - Reduces arthritis and joint pain
🌿 Blood sugar regulation - Helps manage diabetes
🌿 Wound healing - Leaf paste applied topically
preparation: Leaves dried and powdered for long-term storage
Seed oil extracted for topical applications

culturalContext.location: Busoga and Buganda regions, eastern Uganda
culturalContext.significance: Essential in postpartum recovery and child nutrition
culturalContext.traditionalKnowledge: Leaves are pounded with groundnuts for nutritional porridge

moringa_oleifera.seasonalInfo: Harvested year-round, best during rainy season

preparation: Leaves pounded and mixed with honey

culturalContext.location: Throughout Uganda, cultivated in home gardens
culturalContext.significance: Promoted by Ministry of Health for malaria prevention
culturalContext.traditionalKnowledge: Brewed as tea by community health workers

artemisia_annua.seasonalInfo: Harvested before flowering (3-4 months after planting)

medicinalProperties: 🌿 Cold and flu - Chewed bark for respiratory infections
🌿 Antifungal - Treats skin conditions and ringworm

culturalContext.significance: Kept in Baganda homes as preventive medicine

medicinalProperties: 🌿 Digestive aid - Treats stomach problems and worms
🌿 Liver health - Protects and detoxifies liver

culturalContext.significance: Essential in traditional ceremonies and cleansing rituals

medicinalProperties: 🌿 Skin healing - Treats burns, wounds, and skin infections
preparation: Mixed with honey for internal use

culturalContext.location: Cultivated countrywide, native to dry areas
culturalContext.significance: First aid plant kept in many homesteads
culturalContext.traditionalKnowledge: Leaf pulp mixed with turmeric for wound healing
